title: 'The war in Iran is limited to three countries, but the list of collateral victims keeps growing'
subhead: Since the start of the conflict in the Middle East, the specter of powerlessness has affected many countries, and what was supposed to be a regional war has become a global crisis, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

A prominent Palestinian human rights advocate is being barred from entering France
subhead: Shawan Jabarin, the general director of Al-Haq, a West Bank-based NGO that is currently under US sanctions, was scheduled to speak before the European Parliament's human rights committee in Strasbourg this past Tuesday. However, French authorities denied his visa application.

title: Military Advisor to Iran's Supreme Leader Threatens to Sink US Ships in the Strait of Hormuz
subhead: Following Iran's threats, the US announced it was tightening sanctions against Iran's oil industry as the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.

title: At Lyon's Musée des Confluences, the Loeb donation celebrates Mali's living arts
subhead: More than 100 masks, puppets and instruments are displayed in a way that evokes the singing, dancing and movements that once accompanied ceremonies along the Niger River.

title: The Curtis Yarvin conundrum: Should one debate with the far right?
subhead: The reactionary American blogger close to the Trumpian figures was invited to a symposium in Germany with prominent scholars. Some critics say the invitation signals that red lines have vanished from intellectual debate.

subhead: Being a victim is neither a profession nor their identity. Meta, Cristina, Doris, Stéphanie, Mari and Toon live in PFAS contamination hotspots in six European countries.

title: One year ahead of French presidential election, survey shows deep desire for change
subhead: Following the municipal elections, the political landscape is marked by a favorable perception of the far-right Rassemblement National and by highly polarized preferences among French voters, a Le Monde survey shows.

title: New online search tool in Germany reveals if ancestors were Nazi Party members
subhead: In partnership with American and German archives, the weekly 'Die Zeit' has launched a searchable tool granting access to more than 12 million documents, cataloging the bulk of Nazi Party (NSDAP) memberships from 1925 to 1945.
